data.title: Westwood Holdings Group, Inc. reports Q2 2025 results: AUM at $18.3B, Russell 2000 Index inclusion, MDST at $100M, WEBs launches 11 sector funds.
data.description: Total Assets Under Management have increased to $18.3 billion, climbing from $16.8 billion the previous year. WHG has now become a part of the Russell 2000 Index in its 24th year as a publicly traded company. The Enhanced Midstream Energy ETF (MDST) has achieved over $100 million in assets, boasting a 10.2% annual distribution rate. WEBs Investments, collaborating with WHG, has expanded its Defined Volatility℠ ETF Suite by introducing 11 new Sector Funds.

data.title: PetSmart and its subsidiary price $1,950 million aggregate in notes offering.
data.description: Phoenix-based company PetSmart LLC disclosed on August 8, 2025, that it, along with its subsidiary PetSmart Finance Corp., has finalized the pricing for an offering worth $1.95 billion in total aggregate value. This offering represents a significant financial move for the company and its subsidiary.

data.title: Salama's strong H1 2025 performance: Net profit AED 8.25M, Q2 profit AED 7.86M. Equity growth 5.2% to AED 351.84M.
data.description: In the first half of 2025, Salama (DFM: "SALAMA"), a prominent company based in Dubai, UAE, reported a net profit of AED 8.25 million. This growth was primarily fueled by the second quarter profits, which amounted to AED 7.86 million. The company's equity position also showed a positive trend, reaching AED 351.84 million, marking a 5.2% increase from December 2024. The company's improved financial performance underscores its steady progress and strong market presence.

data.title: Core Scientific's Q2 2025 revenue $78.6M, down from $141.1M in Q2 2024. Digital asset revenue $62.4M.
data.description: Core Scientific, Inc. (NASDAQ: CORZ) based in Austin, Texas, released their financial results for the fiscal second quarter of 2025. Total revenue for this quarter amounted to $78.6 million, marking a decrease from $141.1 million in the same period of 2024. Specifically, revenue from digital asset self-mining was reported at $62.4 million, down from $110.7 million in the previous year’s quarter. The primary reason for this decline was not mentioned in the announcement.

data.title: Trump administration imposes tariffs on imported gold; Michael Saylor says Bitcoin is tariff-free in cyberspace.
data.description: The imposition of tariffs on imported gold by the Trump administration has sparked a comparison between physical and digital stores of value by Strategy (NASDAQ: MSTR) Executive Chairman Michael Saylor. In an interview with Bloomberg, Saylor pointed out that "Bitcoin lives in cyberspace, no tariffs in cyberspace," highlighting the advantages of Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) over traditional assets. The decision by the U.S. government to levy tariffs on imported gold bars is causing disruption in the precious metals market. Saylor believes this recent development further solidifies the case for favoring Bitcoin in the current economic landscape. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Clarke Inc. refinanced its construction financing with a $250 million facility and reported a net loss of $0.1 million for Q2 2025.
data.description: Clarke Inc. announced its results for the three and six months ended June 30, 2025. They completed the refinancing of their Talisman development construction financing with a new $250.0 million facility, consisting of a $115.0 million term loan and a $135.0 million construction facility. This allowed them to pay down revolving credit facilities and fully repay a $30.0 million unsecured credit facility. The Company refinanced an investment property in St. John's, NL.  Their net loss for the quarter was $0.1 million and $2.4 million for the six months. Hotel and rental revenue increased due to operations of the first phase of Talisman. The Company's book value per common share decreased by $0.15 during the quarter. The net loss included hotel net operating income of $5.3 million, offset by depreciation, interest, and remeasurement losses on its pension surplus. Full story available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Phunware reports Q2 2025 financial results: $0.4M revenue, $0.6M bookings, 43.9% gross margin. Net loss $3.1M. New CEO appointed.
data.description: Phunware, Inc. reported its financial results for Q2 2025 with software subscriptions revenue down 16% to $0.4 million, but gross margin improving by 1,694 basis points to 43.9%. Net loss was $3.1 million, with net loss per share at ($0.16) in Q2 2025. Net cash used in operations decreased to $6.8 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025. The company debuted hospitality AI features and appointed a new interim CEO. It showcased new AI solutions at a conference and introduced Map Editor 3.0, deploying updates to mobile applications. The company aims to engage in pilot testing for AI products and expand customer base; software bookings for Q2 were $0.6 million. The company is focusing on organic and inorganic growth initiatives with strong liquidity. Adjusted EBITDA is a key non-GAAP measure provided in the financials. Phunware specializes in mobile app solutions and aims to expand its audience and services with generative AI products and enhanced offerings. The company also offers digital advertising and location-based services.
